channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Peru's electoral board confirms June 7 presidential runoff
item.description: Authorities confirmed on Sunday the official results of the first round of the presidential elections in early April, with Keiko Fujimori and Roberto Sánchez advancing to the runoff on June 7.
item.content:encoded:
Authorities confirmed on Sunday the official results of the first round of the presidential elections in early April, with Keiko Fujimori and Roberto Sánchez advancing to the runoff on June 7.

(Image credit: Guadalupe Pardo)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Drone strikes UAE nuclear plant highlighting risk of renewed war
item.description: A drone strike sparked a fire on the edge of the United Arab Emirates' sole nuclear power plant on Sunday in what authorities called an "unprovoked terrorist attack."
item.content:encoded:
A drone strike sparked a fire on the edge of the United Arab Emirates' sole nuclear power plant on Sunday in what authorities called an "unprovoked terrorist attack."

(Image credit: Arun Girija/Emirates News Agency)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Executions nearly doubled in the U.S. last year, and soared abroad
item.description: The number of people killed in state-sanctioned executions worldwide rose to a 44-year high in 2025, according to a new report from Amnesty International.
item.content:encoded:
The number of people killed in state-sanctioned executions worldwide rose to a 44-year high in 2025, according to a new report from Amnesty International.

(Image credit: Curt Anderson)
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise

item.title: What motivated Chinese-born Uyghers to fight in Syria's civil war
item.description: Thousands of Chinese-born Uyghur fighters fled China to fight in the Syrian civil war. They say generations of political persecution in China motivated them to take up arms.
item.content:encoded: Thousands of Chinese-born Uyghur fighters fled China to fight in the Syrian civil war. They say generations of political persecution in China motivated them to take up arms.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: Ukraine conducts large-scale drone strikes on Russia, killing 4 and wounding 12 others
item.description: One of Ukraine's largest drone strikes on Russia killed at least four people, including three near Moscow, and wounded a dozen others, local authorities said Sunday.
item.content:encoded:
One of Ukraine's largest drone strikes on Russia killed at least four people, including three near Moscow, and wounded a dozen others, local authorities said Sunday.

(Image credit: Andrei Vorobyev/Moscow Region Governor Andrei Vo)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The foreign fighters who helped topple Assad — and why China worries about them
item.description: Thousands of Uyghurs became key fighters against Syria's Assad regime. For the first time, they agreed to be interviewed. NPR spent weeks with some of them to understand why they fled China for Syria.
item.content:encoded:
Thousands of Uyghurs became key fighters against Syria's Assad regime. For the first time, they agreed to be interviewed. NPR spent weeks with some of them to understand why they fled China for Syria.

(Image credit: Emily Feng)
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: Bulgarian banger 'Bangaranga' bags country its 1st Eurovision win
item.description: Bulgaria was not among the favorites to win. But performer Dara's catchy, banging anthem bested 24 other nations at the 70th edition of the international singing extravaganza.
item.content:encoded:
Bulgaria was not among the favorites to win. But performer Dara's catchy, banging anthem bested 24 other nations at the 70th edition of the international singing extravaganza.

(Image credit: Helmut Fohringer)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: Iranian man barely survives bombing, but wants war to go on
item.description: An Iranian man who was buried under rubble in US Israel bombing campaign says he wants the war to go on, and destroy the regime.
item.content:encoded: An Iranian man who was buried under rubble in US Israel bombing campaign says he wants the war to go on, and destroy the regime.
item.dc:creator: Durrie Bouscaren

item.title: Life on the edge: why Indonesians living near volcanoes won't leave
item.description: Indonesia has more than 120 active volcanoes. On the island of Java, thousands live alongside Mount Merapi, constantly balancing risks -- and most say they won't leave.
item.content:encoded: Indonesia has more than 120 active volcanoes. On the island of Java, thousands live alongside Mount Merapi, constantly balancing risks -- and most say they won't leave.
item.dc:creator: Naina Rao

item.title: Timmy the humpback whale found dead off Danish coast
item.description: A humpback whale found dead this week off a Danish island has been identified as the animal released two weeks ago in a spectacular and controversial rescue effort after repeatedly becoming stranded off Germany's Baltic Sea coast, Danish authorities said Saturday.
item.content:encoded:
A humpback whale found dead this week off a Danish island has been identified as the animal released two weeks ago in a spectacular and controversial rescue effort after repeatedly becoming stranded off Germany's Baltic Sea coast, Danish authorities said Saturday.

(Image credit: Philip Dulian)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
